mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 225 in CPIAUCSL triggers central bank rate hikes, increasing discount rates and reducing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 225.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in PAYEMS and decline in UNRATE signal tightening labor markets, boosting corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint effect of PAYEMS and UNRATE fails to explain more than 15% of SPX return variance in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating GDP growth (GDPC1) in late-cycle expansions signals robust corporate demand, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns in periods of GDPC1 growth are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) indicates expanding supply capacity, reducing inflationary pressures and supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO fails to explain more than 10% of SPX return variance in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Surging housing starts (HOUST) above 600,000 units signal strong consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 60% of instances where HOUST exceeds 600.

mechanism_narrative: Low FEDFUNDS combined with high RSAFS indicates accommodative policy and strong consumer spending, creating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction between FEDFUNDS and RSAFS fails to explain more than 20% of SPX return variance in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising FEDFUNDS above 4.5% during periods of declining PAYEMS signals monetary tightening and labor market weakness, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5% and PAYEMS declines.
